subject: ap-calculus-ab
title: Particle v(t) = -2 + (t²+3t)^(6/5) - t³ — Speed=2, Position, Direction Changes & Speed Increasing
stimulus: A particle moves along a straight line. For $0 \le t \le 5$, the velocity of the particle is given by $v(t) = -2 + (t^2 + 3t)^{6/5} - t^3$, and the position of the particle is given by $s(t)$. It is known that $s(0) = 10$.

parts.prompt: Find all values of $t$ in the interval $2 \le t \le 4$ for which the speed of the particle is 2.
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: Solve $|v(t)| = 2$ on $[2, 4]$; solutions are $t \approx 3.128$ and $t \approx 3.473$.

parts.prompt: Write an expression involving an integral that gives the position $s(t)$. Use this expression to find the position of the particle at time $t = 5$.
Find all times $t$ in the interval $0 \le t \le 5$ at which the particle changes direction. Justify your answer.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Considers $v(t) = 0$ to find candidates for direction change
1 pt: Justification: $v(t)$ changes sign from negative to positive at $t \approx 0.536$ and from positive to negative at $t \approx 3.318$
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $v(t) = 0$ at $t \approx 0.536$ and $t \approx 3.318$. Sign changes at both values confirm direction changes.

parts.prompt: Is the speed of the particle increasing or decreasing at time $t = 4$? Give a reason for your answer.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Correct conclusion with reason: speed is increasing at $t = 4$ because velocity and acceleration have the same sign (both negative)
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $v(4) \approx -11.476$ and $a(4) = v'(4) \approx -22.296$; both negative (same sign), so speed is increasing.
